uplink.description: 11-in-1 sensor TTN example (battery, temp, humidity, CO2, NH3, H2S)
All twelve channels in order (synthetic in-bounds; expected from running reference/upstream-codec.js, then normalized)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds payload exercising every recognized channel. expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js on these bytes, then normalizing to vocabulary keys (temperature->air.temperature, humidity->air.relativeHumidity, co2->air.co2, battery%->batteryPercent) with the remaining gas/PM channels as camelCase extras under air (PM2.5 keyed as pm2_5).

uplink.description: Negative temperature (synthetic; exercises signed LE decode)
uplink._note: Synthetic payload with int16 LE temperature 0xFFF6 = -10 -> -1.0 °C. expected.data confirmed by running reference/upstream-codec.js.
Upstream silently returns {} (it never sets res.error); our codec diverges per the output contract and reports an error instead of a bare empty object.
